data.title: The WorldCoin team has transferred another 75 million WLD tokens, worth approximately $19.72 million.
data.description: According to OnchainSchool monitoring, after previously transferring 89.65 million WLD, the WorldCoin team's related address transferred another 75 million WLD to another address this morning, worth approximately $19.72 million. These funds may have further flowed to exchanges. Data shows that this address currently still holds approximately 103.9 million WLD, worth approximately $25.76 million.

data.title: Momentum to Unlock 12.53 Million Tokens on April 4
data.description: Momentum (MMT) is set to unlock approximately 12.53 million tokens on April 4 at 10:00 AM UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this token release is part of the scheduled unlocks tracked by the Web3 asset data platform, RootData.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Criticizes NATO Allies Over Security Efforts
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has once again criticized NATO allies for their reluctance to participate in efforts to secure the Strait of Hormuz. According to RTHK, Trump threatened to reduce spending on the protection of other NATO countries. Speaking at a business forum, Trump described NATO's absence as a significant mistake, highlighting that the U.S. invests billions of dollars annually to protect these nations. He suggested that given their actions, continued support might no longer be necessary.

Trump also commented on ongoing tensions with Iran, stating that the conflict is not yet over, with more than 3,500 targets remaining and the mission nearing completion. Additionally, Trump issued a warning to Cuba, indicating it could be the next target following actions against Venezuela and Iran. Earlier this year, the U.S. launched a large-scale military strike on Venezuela and has since increased pressure on Cuba, including implementing new oil restrictions.

Cuban Deputy Foreign Minister De Cossio recently stated that the Cuban military is preparing for a potential U.S. military invasion.

data.title: American Antitank Mines Discovered in Iranian Residential Areas
data.description: American antitank mines were reportedly found in residential neighborhoods in Iran overnight yesterday, according to a post by Aric Toler on X. New York Times posted on X, highlighting the discovery made by Aric Toler and John Ismay. The presence of these mines raises concerns about the safety and security of civilians in the affected areas. The situation underscores ongoing tensions and the potential for further geopolitical complications. Authorities are expected to investigate the origins and implications of these military-grade devices found in civilian zones.

data.title: Global Markets Experience Turbulence Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Monetary Policy Shifts
data.description:
Global markets have experienced significant volatility over the past week due to geopolitical tensions and shifts in monetary policy. According to BlockBeats, the confrontation between the United States and Iran has entered a phase of military pressure combined with diplomatic maneuvering. This has led to restricted passage through the Strait of Hormuz, driving oil prices back to high levels and significantly raising global inflation expectations.

In this context, the Federal Reserve's policy outlook has undergone a critical shift. Several officials have signaled a hawkish stance, prompting the market to quickly move from betting on rate cuts this year to maintaining high interest rates for a longer period, even considering the possibility of further rate hikes. The U.S. dollar index has climbed back above 100, and U.S. Treasury yields have risen, indicating a tightening of global liquidity expectations.

Asset performance has varied significantly: gold has remained volatile at high levels, while oil has emerged as the strongest asset. U.S. stocks have faced downward pressure, with all three major indices posting weekly losses, led by declines in technology stocks. In the foreign exchange market, the Japanese yen has continued to weaken, approaching a critical intervention range, while non-U.S. currencies have generally been under pressure.

Meanwhile, significant changes have occurred in global policy and capital flows. Japan has released large amounts of strategic petroleum reserves and is considering intervening in oil prices through the futures market. Singapore is accelerating its efforts to establish itself as a gold trading hub, and Turkey is heavily utilizing its gold reserves to address liquidity pressures.

Overall, the market has entered a high-volatility cycle driven by geopolitical conflicts, inflation, monetary policy repricing, and asset revaluation. In the short term, the main focus remains on developments in the Middle East and the policy paths of global central banks.

data.title: Mining a single Bitcoin results in a loss of approximately $19,000, prompting mining companies to accelerate their transformation towards AI and computing power businesses.
data.description: With current losses of approximately $19,000 per Bitcoin mined, mining companies are accelerating their transformation towards artificial intelligence and high-performance computing (HPC), and are using the sale of some of their BTC reserves to fund investments in related infrastructure. CoinShares data shows that listed mining companies have announced AI and HPC collaborations worth over $70 billion, indicating a general shift in the industry towards computing power services. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Bitcoin Mining Companies Shift Focus to AI and High-Performance Computing
data.description:
Bitcoin mining companies are increasingly pivoting towards artificial intelligence (AI) and high-performance computing (HPC) due to the current financial pressures associated with mining a single Bitcoin, which faces a loss of approximately $19,000. According to Odaily, these companies are selling portions of their Bitcoin reserves to fund investments in the necessary infrastructure for this transition.

Data from CoinShares indicates that publicly listed mining companies have collectively announced AI and HPC collaboration projects valued at over $70 billion. This marks a significant industry-wide shift towards providing computing power services.

data.title: 21Shares to Distribute Staking Rewards for Ethereum and Solana ETFs
data.description: 21Shares, a cryptocurrency ETF issuer, plans to distribute staking reward earnings from its Ethereum ETF (TETH) and Solana ETF (TSOL). According to Foresight News, TETH will allocate $0.012530 per share, while TSOL will distribute $0.016962 per share. The ex-dividend and record date for this distribution is set for March 30, 2026, with the payment date scheduled for March 31, 2026.

data.title: Whale Suspected of Selling 4,500 BTC Amid Transfers
data.description: A large Bitcoin holder is suspected of selling 4,500 BTC, according to recent monitoring by lookonchain. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the transfer of these Bitcoins occurred eight hours ago, with NYDIG moving the assets to several entities, including Wintermute, Cumberland, FalconX, B2C2 Group, and Galaxy Digital. This movement suggests a potential sale execution.

data.title: Crypto Startups Secure $197 Million Amid Selective Investor Climate
data.description: Crypto startups have successfully raised $197 million this week. According to NS3.AI, the fundraising environment is becoming more challenging as investors are making fewer and more selective investments. Notable companies that secured significant funding include Startale Labs, Ledger, and Tazapay. The capital is increasingly being directed towards stablecoin infrastructure, payment solutions, and enhancing financial connectivity.

data.title: A whale moved 4,500 BTC to multiple market makers, worth approximately $296 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, NYDIG transferred 4,500 BTC to multiple market maker addresses, including Wintermute, Cumberland, FalconX, B2C2 Group, and Galaxy Digital, about 8 hours ago, involving approximately $295.5 million.

data.title: Ju.com's "Meme Second Round IPO Plan" Phase 1, CHIBIPA, will open for subscription.
data.description: Ju.com's "Meme Second Round IPO Plan" Phase 1, CHIBIPA, will officially open for subscription on March 28th at 16:00 and end on March 31st at 16:00. The platform will complete settlement within 4 hours after the subscription ends and complete TGE on March 31st at 20:00, opening the CHIBIPA/JU trading pair. This round continues the multi-currency participation mechanism; users can participate using JU/USDT/BTC/ETH, and the platform will uniformly convert and allocate tokens based on USDT. This round also introduces a dynamic winning rate model. Furthermore, successfully inviting friends to participate will allow you to share up to 20% of the final issuance in token rewards.

data.title: NYDIG Transfers 4,500 BTC to Multiple Firms, Potential Sale Indicated
data.description: NYDIG has transferred 4,500 BTC to Wintermute, Cumberland, FalconX, B2C2 Group, and Galaxy Digital. According to Foresight News, this transaction occurred eight hours ago and may be intended for sale.

data.title: BNB Chain Engages with Blockchain Community at Penn Conference
data.description: BNB Chain announced its participation in the Penn Blockchain Conference, engaging with the blockchain community at the University of Pennsylvania. YZi Labs posted on X. The event spans two days, providing a platform for discussions and networking opportunities within the blockchain sector. Attendees can expect to share insights and explore developments in blockchain technology.

data.title: Altcoin Season Index Declines as Bitcoin Outperforms
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Altcoin Season Index has decreased by four points, reaching a level of 45. According to NS3.AI, the index measures the performance of altcoins relative to Bitcoin, defining an altcoin season as a period when 75% of the top 100 cryptocurrencies outperform Bitcoin over the previous 90 days. This decline indicates that fewer altcoins are surpassing Bitcoin's performance, suggesting a shift in market dynamics.

data.title: US senators have called for an investigation into Bitmain's security risks and its ties to the Trump family.
data.description: U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren sent a letter to Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick requesting information regarding potential national security risks posed by Bitmain and raising concerns about its business dealings with the Trump family. The letter requested the disclosure of communications between Bitmain and Eric Trump and Donald Trump Jr. Reports indicate that the FBI is conducting an investigation known as "Operation Red Sun" to assess the potential risk of remote control of related mining equipment. Previously, U.S. regulators have repeatedly raised concerns about the security of Bitmain's products. Bitmain responded that the allegations are unfounded, and American Bitcoin Corp. stated that its security tests found no issues. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Solana Spot ETFs Experience Significant Net Outflow
data.description: U.S. Solana (SOL) spot ETFs saw a substantial net outflow of $7.8369 million in a single day. According to NS3.AI, the Bitwise Solana Staking ETF was the sole fund experiencing these net outflows.

data.title: Singapore Court Orders Halt to False Claims Against Curve Contributor
data.description: A Singapore court has issued an order to cease false claims and harassment directed at Curve contributor Haowi Wong. According to NS3.AI, the court also awarded approximately $1,900 in damages. This legal action follows a $9.3 million exploit of the Resupply protocol in June 2025, which led some community members to blame Curve and allege the project's direct involvement.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. Intercontinental Exchange invests an additional $600 million in Polymarket, bringing its total investment to $1.64 billion; 2. Trump: Bitcoin holds an important position, and the US needs to maintain its lead; 3. Yi Lihua: The crypto market has been disappointing over the past four years and lacks disruptive innovation; 4. US lawmakers' offices plan to release a draft of stablecoin yield terms next week, and the industry is preparing counter-proposals; 5. Ripple CEO: The Clarity Act may be delayed until May, with approximately $4 billion already invested in the crypto ecosystem; 6. California Governor signs executive order prohibiting public officials from using insider information to participate in prediction markets; 7. US Secretary of State: Iran may decide to establish a toll collection system in the Strait of Hormuz; 8. a16z crypto partner: The crypto industry is entering the "shirt era," and the integration of blockchain and AI will drive on-chain proxy payments; 9. Lido proposal: to use up to 10,000 stETH to buy back LDO; 10. Ripple and Stellar founders invest $1 billion to develop AGI by studying the mechanisms of the human brain.

data.title: Wang Chun withdrew 9,000 ETH from the associated address and deposited them into Aave, bringing his current holdings to 79,818 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, an address associated with F2Pool founder Wang Chun withdrew 9,000 ETH from Binance approximately 8 hours ago, worth about $17.86 million, and deposited it all into Aave. This address currently holds a total of 79,818 ETH, worth approximately $158.7 million.
